samples.xml_description:
Triangle Wave signal generator. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Output waveform signal. State register for controlling the waveform generation. Waveform register representing the generated signal. Reset and counter logic. Describe the design logic.

Reset and counter logic.

Design an 5-bit counter and a state register that is used to control the waveform generation.

Implement the counter and state register in the always block triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

On reset, indicated by ~rst_n, set the state register to 0 and the counter register to 0.

Use a case statement to control the counter and state registers.

When the state is 0, increment the counter by 1.

When the counter reaches 31, set the state register to 1.

When the state is 1, decrement the counter by 1.

When the counter reaches 0, set the state register to 0.

The counter continues to cycle between 0 and 31 based on the state transitions.
